name: Pumpkin Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Line two baking sheets with parchment paper or coat with non-stick spray.
3. In a large bowl, whisk together pumpkin puree, brown sugar, yogurt, and vanilla extract until well combined.
4. Stir in raisins.
5. In a medium bowl, mix together the cake flour, cinnamon, ginger, baking soda, salt, allspice, and nutmeg.
6. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture and stir just until blended.
7. Drop tablespoonfuls of dough onto the prepared baking sheets, spacing cookies about 1.5 inches apart.
8. Bake for 15 minutes or until firm. Remove from oven and let cool on wire racks.
Storage: No-fat-added cookies do not store well. They become stale quickly and stick together if stacked. Once cooled, wrap the cookies in layers separated by waxed paper and freeze. To serve, allow them to thaw at room temperature.
